title: Purpose
content:
1.1 Purpose 
The Board Member Policy shall detail the specific positions available on the Board of Directors, 
their responsibilities, and the election procedures.
sentences:
1.1 Purpose

title: Scope
content:
1.2 Scope 
The Board Member Policy shall apply to all Members of the Society.

title: Policy
content: 1.3 Policy
sentences: 1.3 Policy

title: Chair of the Board
content:
1.3.1 Chair of the Board 
The Chair of the Board shall: 
a.	be responsible for maintaining order during meetings of the Board of Directors, and 
Members; 
i.	attend officer meetings to ensure accountability. 
b.	be knowledgeable of Robert's Rules of Order, the ESS policy and procedures and the 
by-laws; 
i.	conduct meetings in accordance with Robert's Rules of Order Revised. 
c.	be unbiased, objective and impartial for the duration of the meeting; 
d.	maintain all documents of the Society during their tenure as Chair, including but not 
limited to: 
i.	meeting minutes; ii. 	a record of the Board of Directors; iii. 	a record of 
Officers and any changes; iv. 	a record of Members; and 
	v. 	all Instruments of the Society. 
e.	appoint at least one (1) Secretary as per  1.3.2 Secretary of the Board at the first 
meeting of the academic year; 
i.	In the event that the secretary is unable to fulfil their duties at a meeting, the 
Chair shall appoint a replacement secretary for the duration of their absence. 
f.	have other duties and responsibilities as voted upon by the Board for the duration of said 
meeting; 
g.	ensure the agenda is formed and distributed to the Board by a deadline established by 
the Board of Directors at the first meeting of each academic year; 
h.	promote ESS events; 
i.	be informed of any major changes or incidents within ESS; 
j.	be responsible for the accountability of executive mandates; and 
k.	ensure that the Board of Directors meetings and related communications (minutes, 
attached documents, publicity, among others) are accessible to both Francophone and 
Anglophone students.

title: Secretary of the Board
content:
1.3.2 Secretary of the Board 
The Secretary of the Board shall: 
a.	not be a Director on the Board; 
b.	take minutes at all meetings of the Board and of the Members; and 
	i. 	responsible for sending the minutes to the Manager of IT. 
c.	promote the activities of the Board in coordination with the Manager of Social Media, 
including meeting notices and summaries, board members introductions, and updates; d. 
not have voting rights.

title: General Eligibility
content:
2.3.1 General Eligibility 
Any active Member of the Society may run for an Executive position of the Society provided that 
the Member: 
a.	is in good standing with the Society and faculty; 
b.	will be in their second year at the University of Ottawa, or greater, while in office; and 
c.	will be taking three (3) or more classes or on a co-op term in the fall and winter 
semesters.

sentences:

title: Eligibility for President
content:
2.3.2.1 Eligibility for President 
To run for President, an Active Member must: 
a.	have completed at least eight months as an Executive Member of the Society; 
b.	reside in the Ottawa region while holding office; 
c.	meet all criteria aforementioned, or the candidacy will be open in a sequential order; 
 i. if there is no other candidate who meets all requirements in  2.3.2.1.a and  2.3.2.1.b, 
the running will be open to all candidates that have previously served as an Officer of 
the Society; ii. if there is no other Candidate who meets all requirements in  2.3.2.1.d.i, 
the running will be open to all candidates that have previously served as a Director of 
the Society; and iii. if there is no other Candidate who meets all requirements in  
2.3.2.1.d.ii, the running will be open to all Candidates that are an active Member of the 
Society. 
d.	be able to complete the Presidential duties during the entire term of office; and 
e.	must have, or obtain at the expense of the ESS, Emergency First Aid and CPR C or 
higher certification by the start of 101 Week activities. 
f.	in the event of Co-Presidents being elected, they are to share the role and 
responsibilities of the President mandate as stated in 2.3.2

sentences: 2.3.14 Executive Elections

title: Election Period
content:
2.3.14.1 Election Period 
Elections shall be held in two stages, with Presidential elections taking place in November and 
all other executive elections taking place in January. 
Once voted in, the new team will begin shadowing their predecessor until their mandate begins 
in May.
sentences:
2.3.14.1 Election Period 
Elections shall be held in two stages, with Presidential elections taking place in November and 
all other executive elections taking place in January.

Once voted in, the new team will begin shadowing their predecessor until their mandate begins 
in May.

title: Chief Electoral Officer
content:
2.3.14.2 Chief Electoral Officer 
Any person who is not a candidate, official representative, or candidate's volunteer in the 
election can be selected as Chief Electoral Officer. 
Preferably, the Chief Electoral Officer shall not be an engineering student. In the event that such 
a person could not be found, the Officers shall nominate a person who is not an Officer. 
The Chief Electoral Officer shall be paid an honorarium of $150 for by-elections and $300 for 
regular elections adjusted for CPI as of 2024.

a person could not be found, the Officers shall nominate a person who is not an Officer.

regular elections adjusted for CPI as of 2024.

title: Tiebreaker
content:
2.3.14.3 Tiebreaker 
Prior to the beginning of the voting period, the Chief Electoral Officer will decide on a tie-breaker 
candidate in any contested election at their discretion. They will seal the results in an envelope 
to be signed and dated by the President and placed in a locker for which only the Chief 
Electoral Officer will have the combination.

title: Official Representatives and Volunteers
sentences:
An official representative has all the same functions of an election volunteer, in addition to: 
a.	being able to help put posters up; and 
b.	being able to proxy class talks.

title: Candidate Nomination
content:
2.3.14.5 Candidate Nomination 
Any Member who wishes to run must submit form E1 (Officer Nomination) by the date set by the 
Chief Electoral Officer. The nomination shall include 15 signatures of Members of the Society 
and a platform of less than 200 words per language written in English and French. A translator 
may be chosen at the candidate's discretion and is not considered an official representative or 
volunteer. 
Platforms shall be included on the ballot. Before the voting period begins, an email shall be sent 
to all Members, listing all candidates and their respective platforms. 
Any two (2) Members may submit a nomination to be Co-Presidents.

title: Candidates Meeting
content:
2.3.14.6 Candidates Meeting 
A candidates' meeting will be held before the campaign period. All candidates must be present 
unless otherwise arranged with the chief electoral Officer. At this meeting, the chief electoral 
officer will outline the election rules and disclose all penalties associated with infractions of 
these rules.

title: Campaign Period
content:
2.3.14.7 Campaign Period 
The campaign period shall last for at minimum seven (7) business days, including polling days.  
During this period, candidates are required to participate in an election debate between all 
positions and make at least four (4) class presentations in the language of their choice in 
engineering courses or courses with "engineering" in their title at the discretion of the Chief 
Electoral Officer. Candidates running for VP Communications and VP Francophone are required 
to conduct at least one of these presentations in each official language. 
Form E2 (Class Presentation Confirmation) shall be submitted to the Chief Electoral Officer 
before the end of the campaign period as proof of completed class presentations. 
Candidates are allowed to spend a maximum of $50 on posters, banners, or any other 
campaign-related expenditure. The Society shall cover the cost of posters printed using the 
Society's printer, but this expense shall count toward the allowed expenditures described above. 
All campaign receipts must be submitted. 
Physical promotion must follow the following rules: 
a. no more than twenty-five (25) posters may be printed, and they may only be posted in 
SITE and CBY; 
i.	posters must be approved by the CEO prior to posting; 
ii.	posters must be fully bilingual; 
iii.	physical posters must be stamped at the ESS office; iv. 	all posters must be 
taken down after voting; and 
	v. 	violation of the rules outlined above will result in a 5% penalty in votes. 
Digital promotion must follow the following rules: 
a. Social media posts are limited to Facebook, Instagram, and Discord; 
i. 	all posts must be open to the public; 
ii. 	all posts must be bilingual; 
iii. 	all posts on Facebook and Instagram must tag ESS; 
iv. 	all posts must include the ESS logo; 
v.	all posts must be sent to the CEO and approved prior to publication; and 
vi.	violation of the rules outlined above will result in a 5% penalty in votes. 
In addition, a candidate will be disqualified if found guilty, at the discretion of the Chief Electoral 
Officer, of: 
a.	defamation; or 
b.	use of the Society, UOSU or other Recognized Student Government resource not 
available to every student; or 
c.	not submitting a completed nomination by the deadline set; or 
d.	not attending the candidates' meeting without arrangement with the Chief Electoral 
Officer; or 
e.	candidate must not hold any position on the UOSU Executive or have an active 
nomination; or 
f.	slating; or 
g.	any other offence as specified by the Chief Electoral Officer. 
In the event that a candidate is disqualified after submitting a complete nomination, the 
candidate must present themselves to the Board of Directors and, by resolution, be approved to 
run in any subsequent election of the Society. If a candidate in a contested election is 
disqualified after voting has begun: 
a.	and the winning candidate is disqualified, the Board of Directors shall appoint a Member 
of the Society to the vacant office until a By-Election is held; 
b.	in a three-way or more election, if a candidate is disqualified whose number of votes 
could have affected the outcome of the election, the Board of Directors will appoint a 
Member of the Society to the vacant office until a By-Election is held; and 
c.	in all other cases, the election results are upheld and shared. 
A candidate may appeal any penalty or disqualification decision issued by the Chief Electoral 
Officer by submitting a formal appeal within 24 hours to the Board of Directors. If a candidate is 
dissatisfied with the Board's decision, they may escalate the appeal to the UOSU Appeals 
Committee within 24 hours of the Board's ruling. While an appeal is pending, the candidate shall 
remain on the ballot, but the election results shall not be ratified until a final ruling is issued.

title: Voting period
content:
2.3.14.8 Voting period 
The voting period shall be a minimum of three (3) business days. 
Candidates may continue campaigning throughout this period, which shall be considered an 
extension of the campaign period. 
Voting shall take place via a secure, third-party online voting platform. 
A preferential voting system shall be used when the number of candidates exceeds the number 
of available positions. 
If the number of candidates is equal to or fewer than the available positions, a confidence voting 
system shall be used for each candidate.

A preferential voting system shall be used when the number of candidates exceeds the number 
of available positions.

If the number of candidates is equal to or fewer than the available positions, a confidence voting 
system shall be used for each candidate.

title: By-Elections
content:
2.3.14.9 By-Elections 
All By-Elections shall be held between September 15 and March 30 within a period of 20 
business days after an executive position becomes vacant. The By-Election shall be conducted 
with the same procedures as a regular Executive election. The executives, at their discretion, 
may appoint a Member to the executive position until a By-Election can be conducted in 
accordance with this policy.

title: Election Results
content:
2.3.14.10 	Election Results 
The Chief Electoral Officer shall certify the election results and ensure they are submitted to the 
Chair of the Board. Within 72 hours of the end of the voting period, the provisional results shall 
be sent to all Members, clearly marked as subject to ratification by the Board of Directors. The 
Board shall ratify the final results, after which the certified results shall be distributed to all 
Members.

j.	take meeting minutes at all meetings and make them publicly available on their website.

If any of these requirements should not be met a 30% penalty of the Sub-Association's next levy

A Sub-Association may appeal this penalty through application to the Board.

title: Funding
content:
6.3.3 Funding 
Each Sub-Association of the Society shall receive a minimum of 15% of the fee that the Society 
received from UOSU per student in each program of study which the Sub-Association holds 
Sub-Association status for. To be eligible for this funding, each Sub-Association shall complete 
a detailed audit by November 1 of each year, including: 
a.	an audit submission letter; 
b.	the currently approved constitution of their Sub-Association; 
c.	the previous and current year's budget; 
d.	a complete general ledger of the past year; 
e.	all monthly bank statements of the past year; and 
f.	a receipt for every cheque written. 
If the audit is not submitted by the above deadline, a penalty of 15% off the original amount for 
every week late will be imposed. If a submitted audit fails, the Sub-Association will be able to re-
submit without penalty. Failure to provide the above within two months of the deadline results in 
the complete withdrawal of funding.

title: Policy

title: Impeachment
sentences:
An impeachment shall be completed by a two-thirds (2/3) majority vote of those present at a 
Board of Directors meeting.

Any motion to impeach must specify the grounds for such 
impeachment and the specific events justifying the motion while maintaining sensitive 
confidentiality.

The member being impeached must be notified of such a motion no less than five (5) days 
before such a motion is presented.
If an impeachment motion is passed, the member impeached will be removed from office.

The Member will still be permitted to vote at General Meetings, Assemblies, referendums, and 
elections, so long as they remain members of the ESS.

title: Resignation
content:
7.3.2 Resignation 
Any member of the Executive Committee may leave office by addressing a letter of resignation 
to the President. 
In the case of the president's resignation, the letter should be addressed to the VP Internal. 
The resignation of a member takes effect two weeks upon receipt of such a letter. In this two-
week period, the member resigning must work on a transition report for the elected official who 
will take their place.

title: Budget
content:
8.3.2 Budget 
Any Officer may request funds for an event, service or any other project by completing Form F2 
(Budget Line Request) and submitting it to the senior Vice President of Financial Affairs. A 
complete budget detailing the fiscal plans for the year must be assembled by the VPFA, and 
presented for approval by the Board of Directors before 101 week. All budget lines must be 
approved to the extent that: 
a.	the net gain or loss shall be considered the approved amount; 
b.	any net loss less than $300 shall be approved by the Officers of the Society; 
c.	any net loss equal to or greater than $300 shall be approved by the Board of Directors of 
the Society; 
d.	spring and summer expenses shall be approved by the Officers and ratified by the Board 
of Directors at the next meeting of the Board; and 
e.	if a net loss requiring approval from the Board of Directors cannot be approved in a 
reasonable amount of time, the Officers can approve the expense, and it shall be ratified 
at the next meeting of the Board.

title: Expense Reimbursement
content:
8.3.3 Expense Reimbursement 
Any Member seeking reimbursement of approved expenses shall complete form F1 (Expense 
Report) and submit it along with the original receipt of the expense to the Vice President of 
Financial Affairs for approval. If an approved budget line for the expense exists, a 
reimbursement shall be issued for no more than approved. Any expenditures not approved shall 
be owed back to the Society from the Member who made the expense.

locker of the Society.

10.2 Scope 
The Locker Rental Policy applies to all Members and any non-member who uses the society's

title: Rental Duration and Fees
content:
10.3.1 Rental Duration and Fees 
The locker rental period is semesterly or yearly and ends the last day before the next semester 
begins. The cost of rental is to be at least $1 unless otherwise directed by the Board of 
Directors. Fees will be waived for current and past ESS Executives for the duration of their 
undergraduate degree. Upon purchasing the locker, students must identify themselves by 
student card.

title: Liability
content:
10.3.2 Liability 
The Society will not provide a security lock. It is the sole responsibility of the renter to provide a 
security lock. The Society will not be responsible for the loss or damage of the renters' goods 
under any condition.

title: Renewal
content:
10.3.3 Renewal 
The renewal period is within the first month of the new semester. The Society will post an official 
deadline at a minimum of two (2) weeks in advance. Failure to renew will result in the loss of the 
renters' personal lock and locker. Lockers not renewed must be vacated by the last day of the 
renewal period. Items left in the locker will be recoverable for a fee of $5 for a period of one (1) 
month after the last day of the renewal period. Claims for items must be made in person at the 
Society's office. Items left for longer than a month will be disposed of at the discretion of the VP 
Services.

Equipment rental shall be prioritized to Sub-Associations, engineering clubs, and other

title: Inclusivity Policy
content: 12.0	Inclusivity Policy
sentences: 12.0	Inclusivity Policy

title: Purpose
content:
12.1 Purpose 
The ESS wishes to be an inclusive and safe environment for all and, as such, will take action to 
ensure this.
sentences:
12.1 Purpose 
The ESS wishes to be an inclusive and safe environment for all and, as such, will take action to 
ensure this.

title: Scope
content:
12.2 Scope 
The Inclusivity Policy will apply to all ESS events and locations under the authority of the ESS.
sentences:
12.2 Scope 
The Inclusivity Policy will apply to all ESS events and locations under the authority of the ESS.

title: Policy
content: 12.3 Policy
sentences: 12.3 Policy

title: ESS Accountability
content:
12.3.1 ESS Accountability 
The ESS will promote a safe and inclusive environment for all Members of the Society. Thus, 
words or actions that are sexist, racist, homophobic, or otherwise discriminatory will not be 
tolerated. In line with this sentiment, words or actions that are violent, threatening or promote 
rape culture will also not be tolerated.
sentences:
12.3.1 ESS Accountability 
The ESS will promote a safe and inclusive environment for all Members of the Society.

Thus, 
words or actions that are sexist, racist, homophobic, or otherwise discriminatory will not be 
tolerated.

In line with this sentiment, words or actions that are violent, threatening or promote 
rape culture will also not be tolerated.

title: Discipline
content:
12.3.2 Discipline 
If an individual is found not to adhere to section  12.3.1, the steps as listed will be taken: 
a.	on a first offence, the individual should be educated on this policy and why their actions 
are not acceptable by an elected Member; 
b.	on a second offence, the individual will be put on probationary measures; and 
i.	probationary measures include but are not limited to the member being strictly 
overseen by the executives. 
c.	on a third offence, the individual will have their Membership revoked and be barred from 
all subsequent ESS events: 
i.	should a Member wish to challenge this, they will be required to attend an anti-
oppression workshop and make a presentation to the Board about what they've 
learned. The Board will then revisit the membership revocation; and 
ii.	should the individual continue to disregard  12.3.1, a special meeting of the 
Board of Directors will be called to discuss the situation and decide what further 
action should be taken.

title: Procurement Policy
content: 13.0	Procurement Policy
sentences: 13.0	Procurement Policy

title: Purpose
content:
13.1 Purpose 
The Procurement Policy establishes procedures for the acquisition of goods and services by the 
Society to ensure that all purchases are conducted ethically, transparently, and efficiently, while 
maintaining financial accountability and fairness.
sentences:
13.1 Purpose 
The Procurement Policy establishes procedures for the acquisition of goods and services by the 
Society to ensure that all purchases are conducted ethically, transparently, and efficiently, while 
maintaining financial accountability and fairness.

title: Scope
content:
13.2 Scope 
This policy applies to all Officers, Directors, Committees, and Members of the Society involved 
in purchasing goods, services, rentals, leases, service agreements, subscriptions, and 
contractual commitments on behalf of the Society. 
All procurement activities must comply with the Society's financial controls and align with the 
approved budget.
sentences:
13.2 Scope 
This policy applies to all Officers, Directors, Committees, and Members of the Society involved 
in purchasing goods, services, rentals, leases, service agreements, subscriptions, and 
contractual commitments on behalf of the Society.

All procurement activities must comply with the Society's financial controls and align with the 
approved budget.

title: Policy
content: 13.3 Policy
sentences: 13.3 Policy

title: Translation Verification
content:
13.3.1 Translation Verification 
For any procurement involving text content: 
a.	Both the Vice President of Francophone Affairs and the Manager of Translations must 
verify and approve the text before an order is placed. 
b.	Orders containing bilingual text must be reviewed by two (2) individuals at the time of 
submission to minimize the risk of typographical errors. 
c.	If a typographical or translation error is identified after an order has been placed, it must 
be disclosed within 48 hours of recognition, and corrective actions (such as returns, 
replacements, or discounts) must be taken.

c.

be disclosed within 48 hours of recognition, and corrective actions (such as returns, 
replacements, or discounts) must be taken.

title: Vendor Selection
content:
13.3.2 Vendor Selection 
Vendors shall be selected based on price, quality, reliability, and ethical business practices. 
Where feasible, preference shall be given to local, ethical, and sustainable vendors, provided 
they meet the required quality and cost standards.
sentences:
13.3.2 Vendor Selection

title: Conflict of Interest
content:
13.3.3 Conflict of Interest 
Any Officer, Director, or Committee Member involved in procurement must declare any conflicts 
of interest before engaging in a purchasing decision. 
Members shall not personally benefit from the Society's procurement activities. 
Accepting gifts, hospitality, or favors from vendors is strictly prohibited.

title: Financial Oversight
content:
13.3.4 Financial Oversight 
All purchases must be documented and recorded for transparency and accountability. 
Invoices, receipts, and quotes must be retained for auditing and reference purposes. 
The Board of Directors shall review procurement records periodically to ensure policy 
compliance.

Any procurement decision not in compliance with this policy shall be subject to review and 
corrective action by the Board of Directors. 
Violations of this policy, including failure to disclose conflicts of interest or financial 
mismanagement, may result in disciplinary action as determined by the Board.

title: Language Test Policy
content: 14.0	Language Test Policy
sentences: 14.0	Language Test Policy

title: Purpose
content:
14.1 Purpose 
The Language Test Policy shall establish a standardized process for evaluating the bilingual 
proficiency of candidates for positions requiring French-English communication within the 
Society. It ensures that individuals in these roles possess the necessary language skills to 
effectively engage with members, fulfill their responsibilities, and contribute to a bilingual and 
inclusive environment.
sentences:
14.1 Purpose 
The Language Test Policy shall establish a standardized process for evaluating the bilingual 
proficiency of candidates for positions requiring French-English communication within the 
Society.

It ensures that individuals in these roles possess the necessary language skills to 
effectively engage with members, fulfill their responsibilities, and contribute to a bilingual and 
inclusive environment.

title: Scope
content:
14.2 Scope 
The Language Test Policy shall apply to all candidates for positions within the Society that 
require bilingual proficiency. It outlines the assessment criteria, testing procedures, recognized 
certifications, and alternative means of demonstrating language proficiency. This policy also 
establishes the validity period of test results and the requirements for candidates seeking 
exemption through certification or educational proof.
sentences:
14.2 Scope 
The Language Test Policy shall apply to all candidates for positions within the Society that 
require bilingual proficiency.

It outlines the assessment criteria, testing procedures, recognized 
certifications, and alternative means of demonstrating language proficiency.

This policy also 
establishes the validity period of test results and the requirements for candidates seeking 
exemption through certification or educational proof.

title: Policy
content:
14.3 Policy 
Candidates for positions who are subject to this policy must demonstrate French-English 
bilingualism with the following: 
a)	Completing the ESS Language Test, administered unbiasedly by both the VP 
Francophone Affairs and Manager of Translations-or one of them if either position is 
vacant--, the test consists of: 
i)	Part 1: Oral Comprehension & Production Candidates will participate in an in-
person interview designed to verify their ability to: 
1)	Follow and participate in discussions in French/English, including debates 
or role-play scenarios relevant to the position. 
2)	Engage in live interpretation (oral translation), such as reformulating a 
short English message into French and vice versa. 
3)	Communicate effectively, ensuring clarity and professionalism in 
interactions as well as the ability to adopt a more colloquial tone when 
needed.  
ii)	Part 2: Writing Comprehension & Production: Candidates will complete tasks, in-
person, assessing their ability to: 
1)	Write context-appropriate communications in French, including formal and 
informal messages. 
2)	Translate short documents, ensuring the message is clear and suitable 
for its intended audience (e.g., emails, public announcements). 
3)	The evaluation will include materials related to Board of Directors 
activities, formal communications, and informal social media interactions. 
iii)	Minimum Proficiency Requirement 
1)	Candidates must obtain satisfactory results in all required evaluations to 
be eligible for the position. 
2)	The judging panel will assess candidates using the guide in Appendix B. 
3)	This guide will be provided to candidates in advance 
iv)	Results of the ESS language test will be valid for two (2) years following the 
completion of the test and may be used to fulfill language requirements in future 
elections. 
b)	Language Certification: 
i)	A certificate from the University of Ottawa's Official Languages and Bilingualism 
Institute (OLBI) or an equivalent certification recognized by the University (e.g., 
     DELF B2 for French, TOEFL for English) obtained in the past 2 years   ii) 
Applicants who are non-native speakers of both languages may present two separate 
certificates, one for each language. 
c)	Educational Proof: 
i)	Documentation proving the completion of secondary or post-secondary 
education in French or English, either in Canada or abroad. 
1)	Note: Immersion streams will not be accepted. 
ii)	Proof for the non-covered language must also be presented. 
d)	If a candidate submits documentation instead of taking the ESS language test, they must 
also have a conversation in both languages with the VP Francophone and Manager of 
Translations-or one of them if either position is vacant-- to verify their proficiency and 
get feedback on any areas for improvement relevant to the role.

education in French or English, either in Canada or abroad.
1)	Note: Immersion streams will not be accepted.
ii)	Proof for the non-covered language must also be presented.

d)	If a candidate submits documentation instead of taking the ESS language test, they must 
also have a conversation in both languages with the VP Francophone and Manager of 
Translations-or one of them if either position is vacant-- to verify their proficiency and 
get feedback on any areas for improvement relevant to the role.

Appendix B 
Language proficiency assessment guide 
 
The Society will verify bilingualism through the procedure below, provided by the Official 
Languages and Bilingualism Institute (OLBI). 
1.	Oral Comprehension and Production 
Candidates will participate in an in-person interview designed to verify their ability to: 
●	Follow and participate in discussions in French/English, including debates or role-play 
scenarios relevant to the position. 
●	Engage in live interpretation (oral translation), such as reformulating a short English 
message into French and vice versa. 
●	Communicate effectively with members, ensuring clarity and professionalism in 
interactions as well as the ability to adopt a more colloquial tone when needed.  
2.	Written Comprehension and Production 
Candidates will complete tasks, in-person, assessing their ability to: 
●	Write context-appropriate communications in French, including formal and informal 
messages. 
●	Translate short documents, ensuring the message is clear and suitable for its intended 
audience (e.g., emails, public announcements). 
●	The evaluation will include materials related to Board of Directors activities, formal 
communications, and informal social media interactions.
